settings.title: Spring Campaign for Drift Bicycles
settings.summary: We shot a full spring campaign on location that the brand used across web, print, and stores.
settings.description:
Drift was launching a city bike line and needed images that felt like real commutes, not studio setups. They wanted the bikes shown where they belong, on actual streets at the hours people actually ride, rather than against a clean backdrop that could have been anywhere.

The brief

The brand had one launch and three places the images had to land at once: the website, two print ads, and store windows. That meant a single shoot had to produce wide hero frames, tight product details, and square social crops without looking like three different campaigns stitched together.

How we shot it

We planned a two-day shoot across the city, working at dawn and dusk to catch the low, soft light that flatters both a rider and a frame. Three riders gave us a range of real people and real movement, and we mapped locations in advance so each block of light got the shots that suited it.

  • Wide hero frames for the website and store windows
  • Detail shots of the frame, drivetrain, and finishes
  • Square and vertical crops for social
  • A consistent look across the full set

Shooting at dawn and dusk was a constraint as much as a choice. The good light lasts under an hour each time, so we ran a tight shot list and kept the riders and bikes staged and ready, which is how two days covered an entire campaign.

The result

The brand ran the set across its site, two print ads, and store windows, all from one cohesive shoot. Because every frame came from the same locations and light, the campaign reads as one piece wherever a customer runs into it.
